title: calystegia malacophylla var. deltoidea
intro: Calystegia malacophylla var. deltoidea, commonly known as the Island Morning Glory, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Hawaii. It is a type of flowering vine belonging to the Convolvulaceae family, known for its unique appearance and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections. Calystegia malacophylla var. deltoidea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a valuable resource for traditional Hawaiian medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
